content.thinking: Now let me write my detailed analysis text below the dashboard.

summaries.summary: PreparГі anГЎlisis comparativo de estrategias de trading en oro.

content.text:
---

AquГӯ estГЎ el anГЎlisis completo con todo lo que necesitas saber.

---

## Veredicto principal: v1.1 SET 2 gana para prop firm

El dashboard te lo dice directo: **v1.1 SET 2 en forward testing es la configuraciГіn mГЎs robusta**. El Гәnico set donde el OOS supera al IS con ratio 1.18 вҖ” eso es la seГұal mГЎs limpia de borde genuino que puedes tener. v2 se ve mejor en backtesting, pero se desintegra en forward.

---

## AnГЎlisis profundo: ВҝquГ© estГЎ pasando?

**Por quГ© v1.1 SET 2 gana:**
El sesiГіn hour=17, range=4 velas, TP=130, SL=60 sin BE es una combinaciГіn que no necesita condiciones especiales para funcionar. En BK tiene PF=1.141, lo cual parece mediocre, pero en FR sube a 1.343 вҖ” eso es contracorriente y extremadamente raro. Win rate tambiГ©n sube del 35.1% al 38.9% en OOS. Cada mГ©trica mejora fuera de muestra, lo que indica que el borde es real y no era producto de datos vistos.

**Por quГ© v2 falla en OOS:**
El filtro SPS aprende del comportamiento intradГӯa del mismo perГӯodo que se optimizГі. En IS con lookback=20 velas y threshold=0.25, el score se calibra bien porque el perГӯodo de entrenamiento tiene ciertos patrones. En OOS esos patrones no se repiten igual, el filtro descarta trades que hubieran ganado y pasa trades que pierden. El resultado: win rate OOS del 18-24% vs 22-28% IS. TambiГ©n el TP de 395 pips en XAUUSD M5 es un objetivo que pocas velas de una sesiГіn alcanzan, lo que genera muchos trades con BE activado (hasta 19% de los trades en v2 SET 3 FR terminan en cero).

**El problema del BE alto en v2:** Con BE activado a 225-260 pips, el precio primero tiene que moverse 225 pips para activar el BE, y luego el trailing natural del mercado toca el BE y cierra. Muchos de esos trades hubieran seguido hasta el TP de 395 pips si no existiera el BE. En v1 SET 2 directamente no hay BE вҖ” el trade vive o muere por SL/TP puro. Eso es mГЎs limpio.

**Win rate real vs reporte MT5:** Los nГәmeros del MT5 (44-45%) incluyen los trades BE como "rentables" porque terminaron en equilibrio o con comisiГіn mГӯnima. El win rate real (solo trades con profit > 0) es 35-39% para v1 y 18-28% para v2. Para prop firms ese WR real importa porque los streaks de pГ©rdidas consecutivas son lo que mata psicolГіgicamente y genera errores.

---

## Problema crГӯtico compartido: el DD

Ninguna configuraciГіn pasa limpiamente el lГӯmite de 8-10% de equity DD de FundingPips en forward testing. El mejor es v2 SET 1 BK con 8.57% equity вҖ” pero eso es IS, no la realidad. En FR el equity DD sube a 10.76%. La soluciГіn no es cambiar la estrategia: es reducir el lot sizing un 20-30% para que el mismo edge pase con DD controlado.

Con una reducciГіn de tamaГұo de posiciГіn del 25%, v1 SET 2 FR tendrГӯa:
- DD mГЎximo equity: ~7.0% (dentro del lГӯmite)
- Net profit OOS: ~$2,133 en un aГұo вҶ’ retorno ~42% вҶ’ sigue siendo mГЎs que suficiente para pasar el challenge

---

## Pros y contras

**v1.1 SET 2**
- Pros: OOS genuinamente robusto, WR psicolГіgicamente manejable, RR=2.17, sin BE que complique la lГіgica, sesiГіn NY 17:00 es hora de mayor liquidez en XAUUSD, parГЎmetros simples
- Contras: DD en IS supera 10%, necesita reducciГіn de lot para prop firm, solo un trade por dГӯa limita la frecuencia

**v2 (general)**
- Pros: RR excelente (2.42-2.65), DD en IS mejor controlado, concepto SPS es sГіlido en teorГӯa, mayor net profit en IS
- Contras: DegradaciГіn OOS sistemГЎtica en todos los sets, win rate OOS demasiado bajo (peligroso para prop firms con streak limits), TP de 395 pips irrealista para M5, BE demasiado temprano genera muchos trades a cero, Thursday inconsistente

---

## Propuesta para v3.0

BasГЎndome en lo que funcionГі y lo que no:

**Estructura base: v1.1 SET 2 (hora 17, range=4, TP=130, SL=60)**

1. **SPS rediseГұado** вҖ” si se mantiene, usar lookback mГЎs corto (6-8 velas = 30-40 min) y threshold mГЎs agresivo (0.35+) para que filtre menos y solo bloquee seГұales muy claras contra tendencia. El lookback de 20 velas (100 minutos) es demasiado largo y no captura el momentum inmediato al breakout.

2. **Thursday=false** вҖ” el documento v2 lo recomienda y los datos histГіricos son consistentes. Aplicarlo en v3 desde el primer test.

3. **BE adaptativo o sin BE** вҖ” si se usa BE, ponerlo a 50-60% del TP (65-78 pips con TP=130), no al 85-90% como en v2. O simplemente eliminarlo como en v1 SET 2.

4. **TP dinГЎmico por ATR** вҖ” en lugar de TP fijo de 130 pips, usar ATR(14) x 1.5 como TP. En dГӯas de alta volatilidad el target se expande, en dГӯas tranquilos se contrae. Reduce el riesgo de que el TP fijo nunca se alcance en sesiones de rango comprimido.

5. **TamaГұo de posiciГіn basado en ATR** вҖ” SL en pips = ATR actual, no fijo. Con SL dinГЎmico el riesgo por trade es constante en tГ©rminos de volatilidad del mercado, no en pips arbitrarios.

6. **Filtro de dГӯa (expansiГіn de datos)** вҖ” correr anГЎlisis por dГӯa de la semana con los datos que tienes y determinar estadГӯsticamente cuГЎles son negativos. El documento ya muestra Jueves como consistentemente malo. Puede haber otros patrones por descubrir.

7. **ValidaciГіn IS 2022-2023 + OOS 2024-2026** вҖ” el backtesting actual solo usa 2024-2026. Para v3 necesitas al menos 3-4 aГұos de IS para que el modelo sea mГЎs robusto. 2022 y 2023 fueron aГұos muy diferentes para el oro (subidas de tasas Fed, reversiones bruscas).

El objetivo de v3 deberГӯa ser llegar a un forward test con PF вүҘ 1.25 y DD equity < 7% con lot sizing estГЎndar. Con eso, un challenge de FundingPips con 8% target y 8% max DD se puede pasar en 60-90 dГӯas de operaciГіn.

598 lГӯneas, compilaciГіn limpia. AquГӯ estГЎ el desglose de lo que se construyГі y cГіmo usarlo:

---

## Arquitectura del EA v3

**5 mГіdulos integrados en un solo `.mq5`, sin dependencias externas:**

`PlaceORBOrders()` вҶ’ construye el rango, llama al SPS, calcula ATR, coloca las dos Гіrdenes stop con TP/SL dinГЎmicos y expiraciГіn al final del dГӯa.

`CalcSPS()` вҶ’ lookback corto (default 8 velas = 40 minutos antes del rango), recency bias lineal, normalizado a [-1, +1]. BEARISH bloquea BUY, BULLISH bloquea SELL, NEUTRAL deja pasar ambas.

`ManageBreakeven()` вҶ’ se ejecuta en cada tick. Activa el BE cuando el precio alcanza el `InpBE_TriggerPct`% de la distancia al TP desde la entrada. El SL se mueve a `entry + InpBE_LockPoints` (no a cero exacto, sino con un pequeГұo colchГіn).

`CalcLots()` вҶ’ usa `tickSize` y `tickValue` del sГӯmbolo directamente. Funciona correctamente para XAUUSD CFD independientemente del broker.

`OnTradeTransaction()` вҶ’ cancela la orden contraria de forma inmediata cuando una se ejecuta, sin esperar al siguiente tick.

---

## Protocolo de optimizaciГіn en 3 tiers

Todo estГЎ documentado en el header del archivo. El orden importa:

**Tier 1 primero** (80 combos, ~30 min en el tester): encuentra la estructura ORB + ATR Гіptima. VarГӯa `InpRangeCandles`, `InpTP_ATR_Mult` e `InpSL_ATR_Mult`. Todo lo demГЎs fijo.

**Tier 2 despuГ©s** (64 combos): con la estructura del Tier 1 fijada, optimiza el SPS y el BE. VarГӯa `InpSPS_Lookback`, `InpSPS_Threshold` e `InpBE_TriggerPct`. TambiГ©n prueba `InpBE_Enabled=false` para comparar.

**Tier 3 al final** (9 combos): confirma robustez variando la hora de sesiГіn y el perГӯodo ATR. Si el EA es genuinamente robusto, deberГӯa funcionar razonablemente bien en 3 horas diferentes.

---

## Diferencias clave respecto a v1/v2

El **SL y TP ya no son pips fijos** вҖ” se calculan como `ATR Г— multiplicador`. En dГӯas volГЎtiles el target se expande automГЎticamente; en dГӯas comprimidos se contrae. Esto elimina el problema de v2 donde el TP de 395 pips raramente se alcanzaba en sesiones tranquilas.

El **BE ya no es un nГәmero de pips arbitrario** вҖ” es un porcentaje del TP. Si el TP calculado ese dГӯa es $8, el BE al 50% se activa a los $4. Si el TP es $3, el BE se activa a $1.50. La lГіgica escala con la volatilidad.

El **SPS usa lookback corto** (default 8 velas = 40 min antes del rango). En v2 el lookback de 20 velas miraba 100 minutos atrГЎs, captando patrones de horarios anteriores que no tienen relaciГіn con el momentum del breakout. Con 8 velas el filtro analiza el perГӯodo mГЎs inmediatamente relevante.

**Todos los dГӯas estГЎn activos** вҖ” el Thursday=false que apareciГі en los datos histГіricos deberГӯa emerger naturalmente de la optimizaciГіn si realmente existe el patrГіn. No se asume nada de antemano.

content.text:
598 lГӯneas, compilaciГіn limpia. AquГӯ estГЎ el desglose de lo que se construyГі y cГіmo usarlo:

---

## Arquitectura del EA v3

**5 mГіdulos integrados en un solo `.mq5`, sin dependencias externas:**

`PlaceORBOrders()` вҶ’ construye el rango, llama al SPS, calcula ATR, coloca las dos Гіrdenes stop con TP/SL dinГЎmicos y expiraciГіn al final del dГӯa.

`CalcSPS()` вҶ’ lookback corto (default 8 velas = 40 minutos antes del rango), recency bias lineal, normalizado a [-1, +1]. BEARISH bloquea BUY, BULLISH bloquea SELL, NEUTRAL deja pasar ambas.

`ManageBreakeven()` вҶ’ se ejecuta en cada tick. Activa el BE cuando el precio alcanza el `InpBE_TriggerPct`% de la distancia al TP desde la entrada. El SL se mueve a `entry + InpBE_LockPoints` (no a cero exacto, sino con un pequeГұo colchГіn).

`CalcLots()` вҶ’ usa `tickSize` y `tickValue` del sГӯmbolo directamente. Funciona correctamente para XAUUSD CFD independientemente del broker.

`OnTradeTransaction()` вҶ’ cancela la orden contraria de forma inmediata cuando una se ejecuta, sin esperar al siguiente tick.

---

## Protocolo de optimizaciГіn en 3 tiers

Todo estГЎ documentado en el header del archivo. El orden importa:

**Tier 1 primero** (80 combos, ~30 min en el tester): encuentra la estructura ORB + ATR Гіptima. VarГӯa `InpRangeCandles`, `InpTP_ATR_Mult` e `InpSL_ATR_Mult`. Todo lo demГЎs fijo.

**Tier 2 despuГ©s** (64 combos): con la estructura del Tier 1 fijada, optimiza el SPS y el BE. VarГӯa `InpSPS_Lookback`, `InpSPS_Threshold` e `InpBE_TriggerPct`. TambiГ©n prueba `InpBE_Enabled=false` para comparar.

**Tier 3 al final** (9 combos): confirma robustez variando la hora de sesiГіn y el perГӯodo ATR. Si el EA es genuinamente robusto, deberГӯa funcionar razonablemente bien en 3 horas diferentes.

---

## Diferencias clave respecto a v1/v2

El **SL y TP ya no son pips fijos** вҖ” se calculan como `ATR Г— multiplicador`. En dГӯas volГЎtiles el target se expande automГЎticamente; en dГӯas comprimidos se contrae. Esto elimina el problema de v2 donde el TP de 395 pips raramente se alcanzaba en sesiones tranquilas.

El **BE ya no es un nГәmero de pips arbitrario** вҖ” es un porcentaje del TP. Si el TP calculado ese dГӯa es $8, el BE al 50% se activa a los $4. Si el TP es $3, el BE se activa a $1.50. La lГіgica escala con la volatilidad.

El **SPS usa lookback corto** (default 8 velas = 40 min antes del rango). En v2 el lookback de 20 velas miraba 100 minutos atrГЎs, captando patrones de horarios anteriores que no tienen relaciГіn con el momentum del breakout. Con 8 velas el filtro analiza el perГӯodo mГЎs inmediatamente relevante.

**Todos los dГӯas estГЎn activos** вҖ” el Thursday=false que apareciГі en los datos histГіricos deberГӯa emerger naturalmente de la optimizaciГіn si realmente existe el patrГіn. No se asume nada de antemano.

The user is asking which parameters will be optimized. Let me give a clear, concise answer based on the EA I just built.Directamente del header del EA que acabamos de construir, estos son los parГЎmetros a optimizar organizados por tier:

---

## Tier 1 вҖ” Estructura ORB + ATR
*(80 combinaciones В· ejecutar primero)*

| ParГЎmetro | Start | Step | Stop | Valores |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `InpRangeCandles` | 2 | 2 | 8 | 4 |
| `InpTP_ATR_Mult` | 1.0 | 0.5 | 3.0 | 5 |
| `InpSL_ATR_Mult` | 0.5 | 0.5 | 2.0 | 4 |

**Fijado:** `InpATR_Period=14`, `InpSessionHour=17`, `InpSPS_Lookback=8`, `InpSPS_Threshold=0.35`, `InpBE_TriggerPct=50`, `InpBE_Enabled=true`

---

## Tier 2 вҖ” Filtro SPS + Breakeven
*(64 combinaciones В· despuГ©s de fijar Tier 1)*

| ParГЎmetro | Start | Step | Stop | Valores |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `InpSPS_Lookback` | 4 | 4 | 16 | 4 |
| `InpSPS_Threshold` | 0.20 | 0.10 | 0.50 | 4 |
| `InpBE_TriggerPct` | 30 | 20 | 70 | 4 |

AdemГЎs probar `InpBE_Enabled = false` como variante adicional para medir el impacto real del BE.

---

## Tier 3 вҖ” SesiГіn y ATR period
*(9 combinaciones В· confirmar robustez)*

| ParГЎmetro | Valores a probar |
|---|---|
| `InpSessionHour` | 14, 15, 17 |
| `InpATR_Period` | 7, 14, 21 |

---

## ParГЎmetros que NO se optimizan

Estos quedan fijos durante todo el proceso:

| ParГЎmetro | Valor fijo | RazГіn |
|---|---|---|
| `InpSessionMinute` | 0 | Sin variaciГіn lГіgica |
| `InpMinTP_Points` | 500 | Safety floor, no optimizable |
| `InpMaxTP_Points` | 3000 | Safety cap, no optimizable |
| `InpBE_LockPoints` | 10 | ColchГіn mГӯnimo, impacto marginal |
| `InpSPS_RecencyBias` | true | Demostrado mejor en v2 |
| `InpSPS_Debug` | false | Solo para diagnГіstico |
| `InpRiskUSD` | 0.5 | Control de riesgo, no toca el edge |
| `InpOnlyOnePerDay` | true | Regla de gestiГіn, no edge |
| `InpMagicNumber` | 20260404 | Identificador |

---

**Criterio de selecciГіn** al finalizar los 3 tiers: PF OOS > 1.20, DD equity < 10%, ratio OOS/IS > 0.80, mГӯnimo 100 trades en OOS. Cualquier configuraciГіn que pase los 4 filtros simultГЎneamente es candidata para forward testing.

chat_messages.text: puedo optimizarlos todos juntos?

content.text: puedo optimizarlos todos juntos?

chat_messages.text:
The user is asking if they can optimize all parameters together at once instead of doing it in tiers.**TГ©cnicamente sГӯ, pero no deberГӯas.** AquГӯ estГЎ la razГіn honesta:

---

## El problema matemГЎtico

Si optimizas todo junto:

| ParГЎmetro | Valores |
|---|---|
| `InpRangeCandles` | 4 |
| `InpTP_ATR_Mult` | 5 |
| `InpSL_ATR_Mult` | 4 |
| `InpSPS_Lookback` | 4 |
| `InpSPS_Threshold` | 4 |
| `InpBE_TriggerPct` | 4 |
| `InpSessionHour` | 3 |
| `InpATR_Period` | 3 |
| **Total** | **4Г—5Г—4Г—4Г—4Г—4Г—3Г—3 = 46,080 combos** |

Con XAUUSD M5 en 2 aГұos de datos, cada corrida tarda ~2вҖ“5 segundos. EstГЎs hablando de **25вҖ“64 horas de optimizaciГіn continua.** El tester de MT5 puede manejarlo con mГәltiples agentes, pero hay un problema mГЎs serio.

---

## El problema estadГӯstico вҖ” el mГЎs importante

Con 46,080 combinaciones y una sola ventana de datos, **la probabilidad de encontrar una combinaciГіn que funcione por puro azar es casi del 100%.** Esto se llama **data mining bias** o **multiple comparisons problem**.

Si lanzas 46,080 dardos contra una pared, algunos van a pegarle al centro aunque sean aleatorios. El optimizer de MT5 te va a mostrar configuraciones con PF=2.5, WR=65%, DD=3% вҖ” y serГЎn completamente falsas. En forward testing esas configuraciones colapsarГЎn.

---

## Por quГ© el approach por tiers funciona mejor

El tier approach te da informaciГіn en lugar de solo un nГәmero:

- **Tier 1** te dice: ВҝcuГЎl es la combinaciГіn TP/SL/Rango que muestra el plateau mГЎs amplio? Buscas que PF > 1.15 en un rango amplio de valores, no el pico mГЎximo.
- **Tier 2** te dice: Вҝel SPS realmente aГұade valor? Si los mejores resultados de Tier 2 son similares con `InpSPS_Enabled=false`, el filtro no sirve.
- **Tier 3** confirma: Вҝla estrategia funciona en horas distintas? Si solo funciona exactamente a las 17:00 y colapsa a las 14:00 o 15:00, es frГЎgil.

Esa informaciГіn no la obtienes si optimizas todo junto вҖ” solo obtienes un ganador opaco.

---

## La regla prГЎctica

**Si quieres hacerlo de forma simplificada**, el mГӯnimo aceptable es correr **Tier 1 + Tier 2 separados** y dejar Tier 3 fijo en `ATR=14, Hour=17`. Eso son **80 + 64 = 144 combinaciones** вҖ” menos de 10 minutos en el tester вҖ” y te da suficiente informaciГіn para tomar decisiones con fundamento.

Optimizar todo junto es la forma mГЎs rГЎpida de convencerte de que tienes una estrategia ganadora que en realidad no existe.

The user wants to run Tier 1 + Tier 2. They just need a clear, concise protocol to follow in MT5's Strategy Tester. Let me give them a practical step-by-step guide.Perfecto. AquГӯ estГЎ el protocolo exacto para ejecutarlo en MT5.

---

## Tier 1 вҖ” ConfiguraciГіn en Strategy Tester

**PestaГұa Settings:**
- Expert: `ORB_BreakoutEA_v3`
- Symbol: `XAUUSD`
- Timeframe: `M5`
- Date: `2024.01.01 вҖ” 2025.12.31` (IS period)
- Model: `Every tick based on real ticks` (o Every tick si no tienes ticks reales)
- Optimization: `Slow complete algorithm`
- Optimization criterion: `Custom max` вҶ’ si no tienes funciГіn custom, usa **Profit Factor**

**PestaГұa Inputs вҖ” marcar SOLO estos 3 para optimizar:**

| ParГЎmetro | Value | Start | Step | Stop | вң“ |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `InpRangeCandles` | 4 | 2 | 2 | 8 | вң“ |
| `InpTP_ATR_Mult` | 1.5 | 1.0 | 0.5 | 3.0 | вң“ |
| `InpSL_ATR_Mult` | 1.0 | 0.5 | 0.5 | 2.0 | вң“ |

**Todo lo demГЎs SIN marcar, con estos valores fijos:**
- `InpSessionHour = 17`
- `InpATR_Period = 14`
- `InpSPS_Lookback = 8`
- `InpSPS_Threshold = 0.35`
- `InpBE_Enabled = true`
- `InpBE_TriggerPct = 50`
- `InpRiskUSD = 0.5`

Presiona **Start**. Son 80 combinaciones, deberГӯa terminar en 5вҖ“15 minutos dependiendo de tu CPU y agentes habilitados.

---

## QuГ© buscar en los resultados del Tier 1

Cuando termine, ordena por **Profit Factor** y busca **el plateau**, no el pico mГЎximo. Quieres ver algo asГӯ:

```
RangeC=4  TPГ—2.0  SLГ—1.0  PF=1.38  вҶҗ ganador
RangeC=4  TPГ—1.5  SLГ—1.0  PF=1.35  вҶҗ casi igual = plateau вң“
RangeC=6  TPГ—2.0  SLГ—1.0  PF=1.33  вҶҗ casi igual = plateau вң“
RangeC=2  TPГ—3.0  SLГ—0.5  PF=1.41  вҶҗ pico aislado = sospechoso вң—
```

**Regla:** descarta cualquier combinaciГіn que sea el Гәnico resultado bueno en su vecindad. El ganador real tiene vecinos parecidos.

Anota los valores ganadores de `InpRangeCandles`, `InpTP_ATR_Mult` e `InpSL_ATR_Mult`.

---

## Tier 2 вҖ” ConfiguraciГіn en Strategy Tester

Usa exactamente los mismos settings de fecha y sГӯmbolo. Cambia los inputs:

**Fijar los 3 ganadores de Tier 1** (sin marcar para optimizar), y **marcar SOLO estos:**

| ParГЎmetro | Value | Start | Step | Stop | вң“ |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `InpSPS_Lookback` | 8 | 4 | 4 | 16 | вң“ |
| `InpSPS_Threshold` | 0.35 | 0.20 | 0.10 | 0.50 | вң“ |
| `InpBE_TriggerPct` | 50 | 30 | 20 | 70 | вң“ |

Son 64 combinaciones. Cuando termine, repite tambiГ©n una corrida corta con `InpBE_Enabled = false` (fijo) para los mejores 5 resultados de SPS вҖ” asГӯ sabes si el BE realmente ayuda o no.

---

## ValidaciГіn OOS вҖ” paso obligatorio antes de cualquier forward test

Con los mejores parГЎmetros de Tier 1 + Tier 2 combinados, corre **una sola corrida** (no optimizaciГіn) con:

- Date: `2025.01.01 вҖ” 2026.02.28` (OOS period)
- Optimization: **OFF** вҖ” single run

Ese resultado OOS es el Гәnico nГәmero que importa. Criterio mГӯnimo para continuar al forward test:

| MГ©trica | MГӯnimo aceptable |
|---|---|
| Profit Factor OOS | > 1.15 |
| Max DD equity | < 10% |
| Net profit | > 0 |
| Trades OOS | > 80 |
| Ratio OOS/IS (PF) | > 0.75 |

Si pasa los 5 filtros, va a forward testing en demo. Si no pasa alguno, vuelves al Tier 1 con el siguiente plateau candidato y repites.

---

Cuando tengas los resultados del Tier 1 me los compartes y te ayudo a interpretar el plateau antes de correr el Tier 2.

content.thinking: The user wants to run Tier 1 + Tier 2. They just need a clear, concise protocol to follow in MT5's Strategy Tester. Let me give them a practical step-by-step guide.

summaries.summary: IdeГі protocolo prГЎctico para probar estrategias combinadas.

content.text:
Perfecto. AquГӯ estГЎ el protocolo exacto para ejecutarlo en MT5.

---

## Tier 1 вҖ” ConfiguraciГіn en Strategy Tester

**PestaГұa Settings:**
- Expert: `ORB_BreakoutEA_v3`
- Symbol: `XAUUSD`
- Timeframe: `M5`
- Date: `2024.01.01 вҖ” 2025.12.31` (IS period)
- Model: `Every tick based on real ticks` (o Every tick si no tienes ticks reales)
- Optimization: `Slow complete algorithm`
- Optimization criterion: `Custom max` вҶ’ si no tienes funciГіn custom, usa **Profit Factor**

**PestaГұa Inputs вҖ” marcar SOLO estos 3 para optimizar:**

| ParГЎmetro | Value | Start | Step | Stop | вң“ |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `InpRangeCandles` | 4 | 2 | 2 | 8 | вң“ |
| `InpTP_ATR_Mult` | 1.5 | 1.0 | 0.5 | 3.0 | вң“ |
| `InpSL_ATR_Mult` | 1.0 | 0.5 | 0.5 | 2.0 | вң“ |

**Todo lo demГЎs SIN marcar, con estos valores fijos:**
- `InpSessionHour = 17`
- `InpATR_Period = 14`
- `InpSPS_Lookback = 8`
- `InpSPS_Threshold = 0.35`
- `InpBE_Enabled = true`
- `InpBE_TriggerPct = 50`
- `InpRiskUSD = 0.5`

Presiona **Start**. Son 80 combinaciones, deberГӯa terminar en 5вҖ“15 minutos dependiendo de tu CPU y agentes habilitados.

---

## QuГ© buscar en los resultados del Tier 1

Cuando termine, ordena por **Profit Factor** y busca **el plateau**, no el pico mГЎximo. Quieres ver algo asГӯ:

```
RangeC=4  TPГ—2.0  SLГ—1.0  PF=1.38  вҶҗ ganador
RangeC=4  TPГ—1.5  SLГ—1.0  PF=1.35  вҶҗ casi igual = plateau вң“
RangeC=6  TPГ—2.0  SLГ—1.0  PF=1.33  вҶҗ casi igual = plateau вң“
RangeC=2  TPГ—3.0  SLГ—0.5  PF=1.41  вҶҗ pico aislado = sospechoso вң—
```

**Regla:** descarta cualquier combinaciГіn que sea el Гәnico resultado bueno en su vecindad. El ganador real tiene vecinos parecidos.

Anota los valores ganadores de `InpRangeCandles`, `InpTP_ATR_Mult` e `InpSL_ATR_Mult`.

---

## Tier 2 вҖ” ConfiguraciГіn en Strategy Tester

Usa exactamente los mismos settings de fecha y sГӯmbolo. Cambia los inputs:

**Fijar los 3 ganadores de Tier 1** (sin marcar para optimizar), y **marcar SOLO estos:**

| ParГЎmetro | Value | Start | Step | Stop | вң“ |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `InpSPS_Lookback` | 8 | 4 | 4 | 16 | вң“ |
| `InpSPS_Threshold` | 0.35 | 0.20 | 0.10 | 0.50 | вң“ |
| `InpBE_TriggerPct` | 50 | 30 | 20 | 70 | вң“ |

Son 64 combinaciones. Cuando termine, repite tambiГ©n una corrida corta con `InpBE_Enabled = false` (fijo) para los mejores 5 resultados de SPS вҖ” asГӯ sabes si el BE realmente ayuda o no.

---

## ValidaciГіn OOS вҖ” paso obligatorio antes de cualquier forward test

Con los mejores parГЎmetros de Tier 1 + Tier 2 combinados, corre **una sola corrida** (no optimizaciГіn) con:

- Date: `2025.01.01 вҖ” 2026.02.28` (OOS period)
- Optimization: **OFF** вҖ” single run

Ese resultado OOS es el Гәnico nГәmero que importa. Criterio mГӯnimo para continuar al forward test:

| MГ©trica | MГӯnimo aceptable |
|---|---|
| Profit Factor OOS | > 1.15 |
| Max DD equity | < 10% |
| Net profit | > 0 |
| Trades OOS | > 80 |
| Ratio OOS/IS (PF) | > 0.75 |

Si pasa los 5 filtros, va a forward testing en demo. Si no pasa alguno, vuelves al Tier 1 con el siguiente plateau candidato y repites.

---

Cuando tengas los resultados del Tier 1 me los compartes y te ayudo a interpretar el plateau antes de correr el Tier 2.

chat_messages.text: el BE deberia ser "controlado por el ATR"?

content.text: el BE deberia ser "controlado por el ATR"?

chat_messages.text:
**SГӯ, tiene mucho sentido.** Es la evoluciГіn lГіgica del BE adaptativo.

---

## El problema con el BE basado en % del TP

El BE actual se activa cuando el precio recorre X% de la distancia al TP. Pero esa distancia **ya estГЎ calculada con ATR**, asГӯ que indirectamente ya es adaptativa. El problema es mГЎs sutil:

El `InpBE_TriggerPct = 50%` asume que el precio siempre necesita recorrer la mitad del camino antes de protegerse. Pero en un dГӯa de ATR alto (mercado muy volГЎtil), el precio puede hacer retrocesos grandes y normal que activen el BE prematuramente, cerrando trades que hubieran llegado al TP. En un dГӯa de ATR bajo, el 50% del TP puede ser tan pequeГұo que el BE se activa demasiado rГЎpido antes de que el trade tenga espacio para respirar.

---

## BE controlado por ATR вҖ” la lГіgica

En lugar de un porcentaje del TP, el trigger del BE serГӯa un **mГәltiplo directo del ATR**:

```
BE se activa cuando:
  BUY:  precio вүҘ entry + (ATR Г— InpBE_ATR_Mult)
  SELL: precio вүӨ entry - (ATR Г— InpBE_ATR_Mult)
```

Esto significa que en dГӯas volГЎtiles el BE espera mГЎs recorrido antes de activarse, y en dГӯas tranquilos actГәa mГЎs cerca de la entrada. El mercado define cuГЎndo protegerse, no un porcentaje fijo.

---

## ComparaciГіn de los tres enfoques

| Enfoque | Trigger | Problema |
|---|---|---|
| **Pips fijos** (v1/v2) | `entry + 140 pips` | Ciego a la volatilidad |
| **% del TP** (v3 actual) | `entry + 50% Г— tpDist` | Mejor, pero TP ya incluye ATR |
| **ATR directo** (propuesta) | `entry + ATR Г— mult` | Totalmente independiente, mГЎs limpio |

El enfoque ATR directo tiene una ventaja adicional: **el trigger del BE y el SL usan la misma unidad de medida**. Si `InpSL_ATR_Mult = 1.0` y `InpBE_ATR_Mult = 0.5`, sabes exactamente que el BE se activa cuando el precio recorre la mitad de lo que arriesgas. Esa relaciГіn es intuitiva y optimizable con sentido econГіmico.

---

## Rangos de optimizaciГіn para el Tier 2 actualizado

| ParГЎmetro | Start | Step | Stop | Valores |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `InpSPS_Lookback` | 4 | 4 | 16 | 4 |
| `InpSPS_Threshold` | 0.20 | 0.10 | 0.50 | 4 |
| `InpBE_ATR_Mult` | 0.3 | 0.2 | 0.9 | 4 |

Y probar tambiГ©n `InpBE_Enabled = false` como referencia. Total: **4 Г— 4 Г— 4 = 64 combos**, igual que antes.

La restricciГіn lГіgica es `InpBE_ATR_Mult < InpSL_ATR_Mult` вҖ” el BE no tiene sentido si se activa mГЎs allГЎ del SL. Con `InpSL_ATR_Mult = 1.0` del Tier 1, los valores 0.3 a 0.9 siempre cumplen esa condiciГіn.

---

ВҝActualizo el EA con este cambio antes de que corras el Tier 1?

633 lГӯneas. AquГӯ estГЎ el resumen exacto del cambio:

---

## QuГ© cambiГі de v3.0 вҶ’ v3.1

**Eliminado:** `InpBE_TriggerPct` (porcentaje del TP)

**Agregado:** `InpBE_ATR_Mult` вҖ” el trigger del BE es ahora `ATR Г— multiplicador`, igual que el SL y el TP.

**La lГіgica interna del BE nuevo:**
```
slDist       = MathAbs(entry - sl)           вҶҗ SL que se colocГі al abrir
atr_implied  = slDist / InpSL_ATR_Mult       вҶҗ reconstruye el ATR original
triggerDist  = atr_implied Г— InpBE_ATR_Mult  вҶҗ distancia para activar BE
```

No necesita guardar el ATR en ninguna variable global вҖ” lo reconstruye desde el SL de cada posiciГіn en el momento en que la revisa. Esto funciona correctamente tambiГ©n en posiciones que ya tenГӯan el SL modificado (porque compara contra `sl` actual, no contra el original).

**Guardia lГіgica incorporada:** Si por error de optimizaciГіn `InpBE_ATR_Mult >= InpSL_ATR_Mult`, el EA lo detecta, loguea un warning y desactiva el BE automГЎticamente. No crashea ni coloca el BE mГЎs allГЎ del SL.

---

## RelaciГіn visual entre los tres multiplicadores

```
entry в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ BEГ—0.5 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ SLГ—1.0 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ TPГ—2.0
   |_________|_________|__________________|
      50% SL    SL fijo       TP objetivo
```

Con los defaults `SLГ—1.0 / BEГ—0.5 / TPГ—2.0`: el BE se activa cuando el precio recorre la mitad del riesgo, el SL es el riesgo mГЎximo, y el TP es el doble del riesgo. Un sistema completamente coherente en unidades de ATR.

content.text:
633 lГӯneas. AquГӯ estГЎ el resumen exacto del cambio:

---

## QuГ© cambiГі de v3.0 вҶ’ v3.1

**Eliminado:** `InpBE_TriggerPct` (porcentaje del TP)

**Agregado:** `InpBE_ATR_Mult` вҖ” el trigger del BE es ahora `ATR Г— multiplicador`, igual que el SL y el TP.

**La lГіgica interna del BE nuevo:**
```
slDist       = MathAbs(entry - sl)           вҶҗ SL que se colocГі al abrir
atr_implied  = slDist / InpSL_ATR_Mult       вҶҗ reconstruye el ATR original
triggerDist  = atr_implied Г— InpBE_ATR_Mult  вҶҗ distancia para activar BE
```

No necesita guardar el ATR en ninguna variable global вҖ” lo reconstruye desde el SL de cada posiciГіn en el momento en que la revisa. Esto funciona correctamente tambiГ©n en posiciones que ya tenГӯan el SL modificado (porque compara contra `sl` actual, no contra el original).

**Guardia lГіgica incorporada:** Si por error de optimizaciГіn `InpBE_ATR_Mult >= InpSL_ATR_Mult`, el EA lo detecta, loguea un warning y desactiva el BE automГЎticamente. No crashea ni coloca el BE mГЎs allГЎ del SL.

---

## RelaciГіn visual entre los tres multiplicadores

```
entry в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ BEГ—0.5 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ SLГ—1.0 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ TPГ—2.0
   |_________|_________|__________________|
      50% SL    SL fijo       TP objetivo
```

Con los defaults `SLГ—1.0 / BEГ—0.5 / TPГ—2.0`: el BE se activa cuando el precio recorre la mitad del riesgo, el SL es el riesgo mГЎximo, y el TP es el doble del riesgo. Un sistema completamente coherente en unidades de ATR.

This is very revealing. Let me analyze the key findings:

1. **InpSL_ATR_Mult = 1.0 dominates completely** - 98.8% of combos with PF>1.25 use SLГ—1.0. This is extremely clear.

2. **InpRangeCandles**: 7 and 8 dominate (31.4% + 44.2% = 75.6% of top combos). But 4 also has some presence (13.2%).

3. **InpTP_ATR_Mult**: Clear upward trend - TPГ—3.0 is best (39.4%), followed by 2.5 (27.6%). 

Higher TP multipliers consistently deliver better results. The SPS lookback and threshold parameters show almost no variation across their ranges, suggesting they're essentially neutral to performance. Interestingly, disabling the breakeven feature actually improves profitability slightly, while the SPS feature itself appears nearly inconsequential.

The strongest configurations cluster around RangeC values of 7-8 with TP multipliers of 2.5-3.0, achieving profit factors around 1.28-1.31. There's a curious tension here: while BE=false edges out the average performance, the top individual results are dominated by BE=true configurations, which hints that breakeven might reduce variance even if it slightly dampens overall returns. I should investigate whether this pattern holds specifically for the best structural combinations.

Now I have very clear insights. Let me summarize:

1. **SLГ—1.0 is definitively the winner** - 98.8% of top combos
2. **TPГ—3.0 is the clear winner**, with 2.5 also good - strong plateau
3. **RangeCandles 7 and 8 dominate** - but 7 has slightly better DD
4. **BE=OFF actually performs better** on average (PF 1.3075 vs 1.2785), but with higher variance. Without BE: more trades reach TP. 

Looking at the BE parameter more closely, the top results with BE=ON only work because they use specific TriggerPct values in the 55-70% range, while low trigger values (30-45%) actually harm performance significantly. The other parameters like SPS Lookback and Threshold show almost no meaningful variation across settings, and SPS itself performs nearly identically whether on or off at the cluster level, though it's marginally better for drawdown.

The critical pattern here is that BE only helps when the trigger is high enough that the trade has already moved substantially toward TP before protection activatesвҖ”if it triggers too early, it just closes out winning trades prematurely. For the ATR-based BE in v3.1, this suggests InpBE_ATR_Mult should be set higher, closer to the SL multiplier, to replicate that 55-70% behavior. But I'm realizing there's a conceptual issue I need to work through: if TriggerPct represents a percentage of the TP distance from entry, then a 60% trigger with TP at 3.0Г—ATR would put the trigger at 1.8Г—ATR, which would actually exceed the SL at 1.0Г—ATRвҖ”that doesn't make sense.

Actually, I think I've got it now. The TriggerPct is measuring how far along the TP distance the trade needs to travel before BE kicks in, so with TP at 3Г—ATR and SL at 1Г—ATR, a 60% trigger means the trade needs to reach entry + 1.8Г—ATR before BE activates, which is roughly 80% of the way between SL and TP. A 33% trigger would activate at the SL distance itself. So the optimization data is showing that BE works best when it's set to trigger somewhere in that middle-to-high range of the TP distance.

But here's the catch: when I try to map this back to the ATR multiplier approach for v3.1, I'd need InpBE_ATR_Mult to be somewhere between 1.5 and 2.0 to hit that sweet spot, yet the code constraint requires it to be less than InpSL_ATR_Mult which is 1.0вҖ”that's impossible.

Looking at my actual implementation again, I realize the BE trigger distance is calculated as ATR times the multiplier, and the guard ensures BE_mult stays below SL_mult. So if SL is set to 1.0, BE can only range from 0.3 to 0.9, meaning it triggers well before reaching the stop loss distance. But the test results showing optimal performance are measuring TriggerPct as a percentage of the TP distance, not the SL distanceвҖ”so I'm comparing two different reference frames here.

When I convert my ATR-based BE values to this TP-relative scale, the best-performing configurations (55-70% of TP distance) would require BE_mult values that violate my current guard condition. The constraint that BE_mult must be less than SL_mult fundamentally prevents the BE from triggering at the distances that actually work best in practice.

The fix is straightforward: I need to change the guard to allow BE_mult up to TP_mult instead, which would let the BE trigger after meaningful profit accumulationвҖ”somewhere in the 1.5-2.0Г— ATR rangeвҖ”rather than forcing it to trigger too early. This is what I need to explain to Jose: the v3.1 implementation has the guard backwards, and correcting it should unlock the performance gains the optimization data shows are possible. I'm mapping out the specific ATR multiplier values that correspond to different trigger percentagesвҖ”1.5 gives roughly 50% of TP, 1.8 hits 60%, and 2.1 lands around 70%вҖ”so I can now put together the full recommendation with a visual breakdown.
```

1. **SLГ—1.0 is definitively the only winner** - almost 100% of top combos
2. **RangeC 7 and 8 dominate** - 601 and 706 combos with PF>1.30
3. **TPГ—3.0 is the winner**
4. **BE=OFF slightly better overall (PF 1.307 vs 1.278), BUT BE=ON with high TriggerPct (55-70%) is actually better** - the sweet spot is ATRГ—1.65-2.10 (TriggerPct 55-70%)


5. **The critical issue with v3.1** - the current guard condition places the BE trigger in the 30-50% zone where it performs poorly, but it should actually trigger at ATRГ—1.65-2.10 instead, which means adjusting the multiplier range and changing the guard to compare against TP instead.

6. **SPS shows minimal impact** on performance with a slight edge in drawdown control, and the lookback of 4 performs marginally better while threshold variations are negligible. For the out-of-sample configuration, I'm settling on RangeCandles=7, TP multiplier of 3.0, SL at 1.0, enabling BE with a 60% trigger (equivalent to ATRГ—1.80), enabling SPS with lookback=4 and threshold=0.3. There's also a bug fix needed in v3.1 where the BE ATR guard comparison needs to switch from SL to TP multiplier, and the optimization process itself needs adjustment.

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

tree = ET.parse('/mnt/user-data/uploads/Optimizer-11935103.xml')
root = tree.getroot()
ns = {'ss': 'urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:spreadsheet'}
table = root.find('.//ss:Worksheet', ns).find('ss:Table', ns)
rows = table.findall('ss:Row', ns)

headers = [c.find('ss:Data', ns).text for c in rows[0].findall('ss:Cell', ns)]

data = []
for row in rows[1:]:
    cells = row.findall('ss:Cell', ns)
    row_data = {}
    col_idx = 0
    for cell in cells:
        idx_attr = cell.get('{urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:spreadsheet}Index')
        if idx_attr:
            col_idx = int(idx_attr) - 1
        if col_idx < len(headers):
            data_el = cell.find('ss:Data', ns)
            val = data_el.text if data_el is not None else None
            try:
                val = float(val)
            except:
                pass
            row_data[headers[col_idx]] = val
        col_idx += 1
    data.append(row_data)

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
print(f"Shape: {df.shape}")
print(f"\nColumns: {list(df.columns)}")
print(f"\nNumeric stats:")
print(df[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','Recovery Factor','Sharpe Ratio']].describe().round(3))

# Parameter ranges actually tested
print("\nParameter ranges in data:")
for col in ['InpRangeCandles','InpTP_ATR_Mult','InpSL_ATR_Mult','InpBE_Enabled','InpBE_TriggerPct','InpSPS_Enabled','InpSPS_Lookback','InpSPS_Threshold']:
    if col in df.columns:
        vals = sorted(df[col].dropna().unique())
        print(f"  {col}: {vals}")

df.to_csv('/home/claude/optimizer_results.csv', index=False)
print("\nSaved to CSV")
EOF

    row_data = {}
    col_idx = 0
    for cell in cells:
        idx_attr = cell.get('{urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:spreadsheet}Index')
        if idx_attr:
            col_idx = int(idx_attr) - 1
        if col_idx < len(headers):
            data_el = cell.find('ss:Data', ns)
            val = data_el.text if data_el is not None else None
            try:
                val = float(val)
            except:
                pass
            row_data[headers[col_idx]] = val
        col_idx += 1
    data.append(row_data)

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
print(f"Shape: {df.shape}")
print(f"\nColumns: {list(df.columns)}")
print(f"\nNumeric stats:")
print(df[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','Recovery Factor','Sharpe Ratio']].describe().round(3))

# Parameter ranges actually tested
print("\nParameter ranges in data:")
for col in ['InpRangeCandles','InpTP_ATR_Mult','InpSL_ATR_Mult','InpBE_Enabled','InpBE_TriggerPct','InpSPS_Enabled','InpSPS_Lookback','InpSPS_Threshold']:
    if col in df.columns:
        vals = sorted(df[col].dropna().unique())
        print(f"  {col}: {vals}")

df.to_csv('/home/claude/optimizer_results.csv', index=False)
print("\nSaved to CSV")
EOF
"}

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv('/home/claude/optimizer_results.csv')

viable = df[
    (df['Profit Factor'] > 1.10) &
    (df['Equity DD %'] < 10.0) &
    (df['Trades'] >= 100) &
    (df['Profit'] > 0)
].copy()

# в”Җв”Җ 1. PLATEAU: media PF por parГЎmetro individual в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("=== PLATEAU POR PARГҒMETRO (PF medio) ===")
for col, vals in [
    ('InpRangeCandles', [2,3,4,5,6,7,8]),
    ('InpTP_ATR_Mult',  [1.0,1.5,2.0,2.5,3.0]),
    ('InpSL_ATR_Mult',  [0.5,1.0,1.5,2.0]),
    ('InpSPS_Lookback', [4,8,12,16]),
    ('InpSPS_Threshold',[0.2,0.3,0.4,0.5]),
    ('InpBE_TriggerPct',[30,35,40,45,50,55,60,65,70]),
]:
    print(f"\n{col}:")
    for v in vals:
        sub = viable[viable[col] == v]
        if len(sub) > 0:
            print(f"  {v:6.1f} вҶ’ PF={sub['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={sub['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  Trades={sub['Trades'].mean():.0f}  n={len(sub)}")

# в”Җв”Җ 2. BE ON vs OFF в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("\n=== BE ENABLED vs DISABLED ===")
for be_val in ['true','false']:
    sub = viable[viable['InpBE_Enabled'].astype(str).str.lower() == be_val]
    print(f"  BE={be_val}: PF={sub['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={sub['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  n={len(sub)}")

# в”Җв”Җ 3. SPS ON vs OFF в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("\n=== SPS ENABLED vs DISABLED ===")
for sps_val in ['true','false']:
    sub = viable[viable['InpSPS_Enabled'].astype(str).str.lower() == sps_val]
    print(f"  SPS={sps_val}: PF={sub['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={sub['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  n={len(sub)}")

# в”Җв”Җ 4. CombinaciГіn ganadora estructural в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("\n=== TOP COMBINACIONES ESTRUCTURALES (RangeC, TP_mult, SL_mult) ===")
grp = viable.groupby(['InpRangeCandles','InpTP_ATR_Mult','InpSL_ATR_Mult']).agg(
    PF_mean=('Profit Factor','mean'),
    PF_max=('Profit Factor','max'),
    DD_mean=('Equity DD %','mean'),
    Trades_mean=('Trades','mean'),
    n=('Profit Factor','count')
).reset_index().sort_values('PF_mean', ascending=False).head(20)
print(grp.to_string(index=False))

# в”Җв”Җ 5. AnГЎlisis de robustez del plateau в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("\n=== ROBUSTEZ: combos con PF>1.25 вҖ” distribuciГіn de parГЎmetros ===")
top = viable[viable['Profit Factor'] >= 1.25]
for col in ['InpRangeCandles','InpTP_ATR_Mult','InpSL_ATR_Mult','InpSPS_Lookback','InpSPS_Threshold']:
    vc = top[col].value_counts().sort_index()
    print(f"\n{col}:")
    for k,v in vc.items():
        pct = v/len(top)*100
        bar = 'в–Ҳ' * int(pct/2)
        print(f"  {k:5.1f}: {bar} {pct:.1f}% ({v})")

EOF

content.thinking:
This is very revealing. Let me analyze the key findings:

1. **InpSL_ATR_Mult = 1.0 dominates completely** - 98.8% of combos with PF>1.25 use SLГ—1.0. This is extremely clear.

2. **InpRangeCandles**: 7 and 8 dominate (31.4% + 44.2% = 75.6% of top combos). But 4 also has some presence (13.2%).

3. **InpTP_ATR_Mult**: Clear upward trend - TPГ—3.0 is best (39.4%), followed by 2.5 (27.6%). 

Higher TP multipliers consistently deliver better results. The SPS lookback and threshold parameters show almost no variation across their ranges, suggesting they're essentially neutral to performance. Interestingly, disabling the breakeven feature actually improves profitability slightly, while the SPS feature itself appears nearly inconsequential.

The strongest configurations cluster around RangeC values of 7-8 with TP multipliers of 2.5-3.0, achieving profit factors around 1.28-1.31. There's a curious tension here: while BE=false edges out the average performance, the top individual results are dominated by BE=true configurations, which hints that breakeven might reduce variance even if it slightly dampens overall returns. I should investigate whether this pattern holds specifically for the best structural combinations.

summaries.summary: Dissecting parameter dominance and range candle patterns.
Analyzed trading strategy parameters and identified dominant performance factors.

python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv('/home/claude/optimizer_results.csv')
df['BE_bool'] = df['InpBE_Enabled'].astype(str).str.lower() == 'true'
df['SPS_bool'] = df['InpSPS_Enabled'].astype(str).str.lower() == 'true'

viable = df[
    (df['Profit Factor'] > 1.10) &
    (df['Equity DD %'] < 10.0) &
    (df['Trades'] >= 100) &
    (df['Profit'] > 0)
].copy()

# в”Җв”Җ CANDIDATO GANADOR: RangeC=7, TPГ—3.0, SLГ—1.0 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("=== ANГҒLISIS PROFUNDO: RangeCвҲҲ{7,8}, TPГ—3.0, SLГ—1.0 ===")
core = viable[
    (viable['InpRangeCandles'].isin([7.0, 8.0])) &
    (viable['InpTP_ATR_Mult'] == 3.0) &
    (viable['InpSL_ATR_Mult'] == 1.0)
]
print(f"Total combos en este cluster: {len(core)}")
print(f"\nPF range: {core['Profit Factor'].min():.4f} вҖ“ {core['Profit Factor'].max():.4f}")
print(f"DD range: {core['Equity DD %'].min():.2f}% вҖ“ {core['Equity DD %'].max():.2f}%")
print(f"Profit range: ${core['Profit'].min():.0f} вҖ“ ${core['Profit'].max():.0f}")

# BE ON vs OFF dentro del cluster ganador
print("\n--- BE dentro del cluster ganador ---")
for be in [True, False]:
    s = core[core['BE_bool'] == be]
    print(f"  BE={'ON' if be else 'OFF'}: PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  Profit=${s['Profit'].mean():.0f}  n={len(s)}")

# SPS ON vs OFF
print("\n--- SPS dentro del cluster ganador ---")
for sps in [True, False]:
    s = core[core['SPS_bool'] == sps]
    print(f"  SPS={'ON' if sps else 'OFF'}: PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  Profit=${s['Profit'].mean():.0f}  n={len(s)}")

# BE TriggerPct dentro del cluster
print("\n--- InpBE_TriggerPct dentro del cluster (solo BE=ON) ---")
core_be = core[core['BE_bool']]
for v in sorted(core_be['InpBE_TriggerPct'].unique()):
    s = core_be[core_be['InpBE_TriggerPct'] == v]
    print(f"  TriggerPct={v:.0f}%: PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  n={len(s)}")

# SPS Lookback dentro del cluster
print("\n--- SPS Lookback + Threshold (SPS=ON) ---")
core_sps = core[core['SPS_bool']]
for lb in sorted(core_sps['InpSPS_Lookback'].unique()):
    s = core_sps[core_sps['InpSPS_Lookback'] == lb]
    print(f"  Lookback={lb:.0f}: PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  n={len(s)}")

print()
for th in sorted(core_sps['InpSPS_Threshold'].unique()):
    s = core_sps[core_sps['InpSPS_Threshold'] == th]
    print(f"  Threshold={th:.1f}: PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  n={len(s)}")

# в”Җв”Җ TOP 5 configuraciones finales del cluster в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("\n=== TOP 10 CONFIGURACIONES DENTRO DEL CLUSTER ===")
top_core = core.sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False).head(10)
cols = ['Pass','Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','Sharpe Ratio',
        'InpRangeCandles','InpTP_ATR_Mult','InpSL_ATR_Mult',
        'InpBE_Enabled','InpBE_TriggerPct','InpSPS_Enabled','InpSPS_Lookback','InpSPS_Threshold']
print(top_core[cols].to_string(index=False))

# в”Җв”Җ CONFIGURACIГ“N RECOMENDADA FINAL в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("\n=== CONFIGURACIГ“N RECOMENDADA PARA OOS TEST ===")

{"language": "bash", "code": "python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv('/home/claude/optimizer_results.csv')
df['BE_bool'] = df['InpBE_Enabled'].astype(str).str.lower() == 'true'
df['SPS_bool'] = df['InpSPS_Enabled'].astype(str).str.lower() == 'true'

viable = df[
    (df['Profit Factor'] > 1.10) &
    (df['Equity DD %'] < 10.0) &
    (df['Trades'] >= 100) &
    (df['Profit'] > 0)
].copy()

# ── CANDIDATO GANADOR: RangeC=7, TP×3.0, SL×1.0 ──────────────────
print("=== ANÁLISIS PROFUNDO: RangeC∈{7,8}, TP×3.0, SL×1.0 ===")
core = viable[
    (viable['InpRangeCandles'].isin([7.0, 8.0])) &
    (viable['InpTP_ATR_Mult'] == 3.0) &
    (viable['InpSL_ATR_Mult'] == 1.0)
]
print(f"Total combos en este cluster: {len(core)}")
print(f"\nPF range: {core['Profit Factor'].min():.4f} – {core['Profit Factor'].max():.4f}")
print(f"DD range: {core['Equity DD %'].min():.2f}% – {core['Equity DD %'].max():.2f}%")
print(f"Profit range: ${core['Profit'].min():.0f} – ${core['Profit'].max():.0f}")

# BE ON vs OFF dentro del cluster ganador
print("\n--- BE dentro del cluster ganador ---")
for be in [True, False]:
    s = core[core['BE_bool'] == be]
    print(f"  BE={'ON' if be else 'OFF'}: PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  Profit=${s['Profit'].mean():.0f}  n={len(s)}")

# SPS ON vs OFF
print("\n--- SPS dentro del cluster ganador ---")
for sps in [True, False]:
    s = core[core['SPS_bool'] == sps]
    print(f"  SPS={'ON' if sps else 'OFF'}: PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  Profit=${s['Profit'].mean():.0f}  n={len(s)}")

# BE TriggerPct dentro del cluster
print("\n--- InpBE_TriggerPct dentro del cluster (solo BE=ON) ---")
core_be = core[core['BE_bool']]
for v in sorted(core_be['InpBE_TriggerPct'].unique()):
    s = core_be[core_be['InpBE_TriggerPct'] == v]
    print(f"  TriggerPct={v:.0f}%: PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  n={len(s)}")

# SPS Lookback dentro del cluster
print("\n--- SPS Lookback + Threshold (SPS=ON) ---")
core_sps = core[core['SPS_bool']]
for lb in sorted(core_sps['InpSPS_Lookback'].unique()):
    s = core_sps[core_sps['InpSPS_Lookback'] == lb]
    print(f"  Lookback={lb:.0f}: PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  n={len(s)}")

print()
for th in sorted(core_sps['InpSPS_Threshold'].unique()):
    s = core_sps[core_sps['InpSPS_Threshold'] == th]
    print(f"  Threshold={th:.1f}: PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  n={len(s)}")

# ── TOP 5 configuraciones finales del cluster ─────────────────────
print("\n=== TOP 10 CONFIGURACIONES DENTRO DEL CLUSTER ===")
top_core = core.sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False).head(10)
cols = ['Pass','Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','Sharpe Ratio',
        'InpRangeCandles','InpTP_ATR_Mult','InpSL_ATR_Mult',
        'InpBE_Enabled','InpBE_TriggerPct','InpSPS_Enabled','InpSPS_Lookback','InpSPS_Threshold']
print(top_core[cols].to_string(index=False))

# ── CONFIGURACIÓN RECOMENDADA FINAL ──────────────────────────────
print("\n=== CONFIGURACIÓN RECOMENDADA PARA OOS TEST ===")
# Buscar el centro del plateau: RangeC=7, TP×3.0, SL×1.0, BE=OFF (ligeramente mejor), SPS=ON con threshold conservador
best_center = core[
    (core['InpRangeCandles'] == 7.0) &
    (core['InpTP_ATR_Mult'] == 3.0) &
    (core['InpSL_ATR_Mult'] == 1.0) &
    (core['SPS_bool'] == True) &
    (core['InpSPS_Lookback'] == 4.0) &
    (core['InpSPS_Threshold'] == 0.3)
]
print(f"RangeC=7, TP×3.0, SL×1.0, SPS=ON, Lookback=4, Threshold=0.3:")
print(f"  Combos: {len(best_center)}")
if len(best_center) > 0:
    print(f"  PF: {best_center['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f} (mean)  max={best_center['Profit Factor'].max():.4f}")
    print(f"  DD: {best_center['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%")
    print(f"  Trades: {best_center['Trades'].mean():.0f}")
    print(f"  Profit: ${best_center['Profit'].mean():.0f}")
EOF

content.thinking:
Now I have very clear insights. Let me summarize:

1. **SLГ—1.0 is definitively the winner** - 98.8% of top combos
2. **TPГ—3.0 is the clear winner**, with 2.5 also good - strong plateau
3. **RangeCandles 7 and 8 dominate** - but 7 has slightly better DD
4. **BE=OFF actually performs better** on average (PF 1.3075 vs 1.2785), but with higher variance. Without BE: more trades reach TP. 

Looking at the BE parameter more closely, the top results with BE=ON only work because they use specific TriggerPct values in the 55-70% range, while low trigger values (30-45%) actually harm performance significantly. The other parameters like SPS Lookback and Threshold show almost no meaningful variation across settings, and SPS itself performs nearly identically whether on or off at the cluster level, though it's marginally better for drawdown.

The critical pattern here is that BE only helps when the trigger is high enough that the trade has already moved substantially toward TP before protection activatesвҖ”if it triggers too early, it just closes out winning trades prematurely. For the ATR-based BE in v3.1, this suggests InpBE_ATR_Mult should be set higher, closer to the SL multiplier, to replicate that 55-70% behavior. But I'm realizing there's a conceptual issue I need to work through: if TriggerPct represents a percentage of the TP distance from entry, then a 60% trigger with TP at 3.0Г—ATR would put the trigger at 1.8Г—ATR, which would actually exceed the SL at 1.0Г—ATRвҖ”that doesn't make sense.

Actually, I think I've got it now. The TriggerPct is measuring how far along the TP distance the trade needs to travel before BE kicks in, so with TP at 3Г—ATR and SL at 1Г—ATR, a 60% trigger means the trade needs to reach entry + 1.8Г—ATR before BE activates, which is roughly 80% of the way between SL and TP. A 33% trigger would activate at the SL distance itself. So the optimization data is showing that BE works best when it's set to trigger somewhere in that middle-to-high range of the TP distance.

But here's the catch: when I try to map this back to the ATR multiplier approach for v3.1, I'd need InpBE_ATR_Mult to be somewhere between 1.5 and 2.0 to hit that sweet spot, yet the code constraint requires it to be less than InpSL_ATR_Mult which is 1.0вҖ”that's impossible.

Looking at my actual implementation again, I realize the BE trigger distance is calculated as ATR times the multiplier, and the guard ensures BE_mult stays below SL_mult. So if SL is set to 1.0, BE can only range from 0.3 to 0.9, meaning it triggers well before reaching the stop loss distance. But the test results showing optimal performance are measuring TriggerPct as a percentage of the TP distance, not the SL distanceвҖ”so I'm comparing two different reference frames here.

When I convert my ATR-based BE values to this TP-relative scale, the best-performing configurations (55-70% of TP distance) would require BE_mult values that violate my current guard condition. The constraint that BE_mult must be less than SL_mult fundamentally prevents the BE from triggering at the distances that actually work best in practice.

The fix is straightforward: I need to change the guard to allow BE_mult up to TP_mult instead, which would let the BE trigger after meaningful profit accumulationвҖ”somewhere in the 1.5-2.0Г— ATR rangeвҖ”rather than forcing it to trigger too early. This is what I need to explain to Jose: the v3.1 implementation has the guard backwards, and correcting it should unlock the performance gains the optimization data shows are possible. I'm mapping out the specific ATR multiplier values that correspond to different trigger percentagesвҖ”1.5 gives roughly 50% of TP, 1.8 hits 60%, and 2.1 lands around 70%вҖ”so I can now put together the full recommendation with a visual breakdown.

summaries.summary: Synthesizing optimal parameters across trading strategy configurations.
Reconciling ATR-based BE logic with optimization findings.
Scrutinizing v3.1 BE implementation constraints and architecture flaws.
Excavated optimization patterns and identified critical implementation flaw.

python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv('/home/claude/optimizer_results.csv')
df['BE_bool'] = df['InpBE_Enabled'].astype(str).str.lower() == 'true'

# Insight crГӯtico: BE TriggerPct expresado como mГәltiplo de ATR
# Con TP=3.0xATR, el TriggerPct% de TP equivale a: pct/100 * 3.0 = BE_ATR_equivalent
print("=== EQUIVALENCIA BE_TriggerPct вҶ’ ATR_mult (con TPГ—3.0) ===")
pcts = [30,35,40,45,50,55,60,65,70]
for p in pcts:
    atr_eq = p/100 * 3.0
    print(f"  TriggerPct={p}% вҶ’ ATR_mult_equivalente={atr_eq:.2f}  (referencia: SL=1.0xATR)")

# Pivot: por cada combo RangeC=7, TPГ—3.0, SLГ—1.0, BE=ON
# mostrar PF por TriggerPct вҖ” confirmar el sweet spot
core_be = df[
    (df['InpRangeCandles'] == 7.0) &
    (df['InpTP_ATR_Mult'] == 3.0) &
    (df['InpSL_ATR_Mult'] == 1.0) &
    (df['BE_bool'] == True) &
    (df['Equity DD %'] < 10.0) &
    (df['Profit Factor'] > 1.0)
]
print(f"\n=== BE TRIGGER SWEET SPOT (RangeC=7, TPГ—3.0, SLГ—1.0, BE=ON) ===")
for v in sorted(core_be['InpBE_TriggerPct'].unique()):
    s = core_be[core_be['InpBE_TriggerPct'] == v]
    atr_eq = v/100 * 3.0
    bar = 'в–Ҳ' * int(s['Profit Factor'].mean() * 10 - 10)
    print(f"  Pct={v:3.0f}% (ATRГ—{atr_eq:.2f}): PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  {bar}")

# NГәmero de combos con PF>1.30 por RangeCandles
print("\n=== COMBOS CON PF>1.30 POR RANGEC ===")
top30 = df[(df['Profit Factor']>1.30)&(df['Equity DD %']<10)&(df['Trades']>100)&(df['Profit']>0)]
for rc in [2,3,4,5,6,7,8]:
    n = len(top30[top30['InpRangeCandles']==rc])
    bar = 'в–Ҳ' * n
    print(f"  RangeC={rc}: {bar} ({n})")

# Mejor config sin BE (BE=OFF ya es superior globalmente)
print("\n=== TOP CONFIGS BE=OFF ===")
be_off = df[
    (df['BE_bool'] == False) &
    (df['Equity DD %'] < 10.0) &
    (df['Profit Factor'] > 1.0) &
    (df['Trades'] >= 100)
].sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False).head(10)
cols = ['Pass','Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades',
        'InpRangeCandles','InpTP_ATR_Mult','InpSL_ATR_Mult',
        'InpSPS_Enabled','InpSPS_Lookback','InpSPS_Threshold']
print(be_off[cols].to_string(index=False))

EOF

    atr_eq = v/100 * 3.0
    bar = '█' * int(s['Profit Factor'].mean() * 10 - 10)
    print(f"  Pct={v:3.0f}% (ATR×{atr_eq:.2f}): PF={s['Profit Factor'].mean():.4f}  DD={s['Equity DD %'].mean():.2f}%  {bar}")

# Número de combos con PF>1.30 por RangeCandles
print("\n=== COMBOS CON PF>1.30 POR RANGEC ===")
top30 = df[(df['Profit Factor']>1.30)&(df['Equity DD %']<10)&(df['Trades']>100)&(df['Profit']>0)]
for rc in [2,3,4,5,6,7,8]:
    n = len(top30[top30['InpRangeCandles']==rc])
    bar = '█' * n
    print(f"  RangeC={rc}: {bar} ({n})")

# Mejor config sin BE (BE=OFF ya es superior globalmente)
print("\n=== TOP CONFIGS BE=OFF ===")
be_off = df[
    (df['BE_bool'] == False) &
    (df['Equity DD %'] < 10.0) &
    (df['Profit Factor'] > 1.0) &
    (df['Trades'] >= 100)
].sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False).head(10)
cols = ['Pass','Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades',
        'InpRangeCandles','InpTP_ATR_Mult','InpSL_ATR_Mult',
        'InpSPS_Enabled','InpSPS_Lookback','InpSPS_Threshold']
print(be_off[cols].to_string(index=False))

EOF
"}

content.thinking:
Now I have extremely clear insights. Let me build the full visualization dashboard.

Key findings:
1. **SLГ—1.0 is definitively the only winner** - almost 100% of top combos
2. **RangeC 7 and 8 dominate** - 601 and 706 combos with PF>1.30
3. **TPГ—3.0 is the winner**
4. **BE=OFF slightly better overall (PF 1.307 vs 1.278), BUT BE=ON with high TriggerPct (55-70%) is actually better** - the sweet spot is ATRГ—1.65-2.10 (TriggerPct 55-70%)


5. **The critical issue with v3.1** - the current guard condition places the BE trigger in the 30-50% zone where it performs poorly, but it should actually trigger at ATRГ—1.65-2.10 instead, which means adjusting the multiplier range and changing the guard to compare against TP instead.

6. **SPS shows minimal impact** on performance with a slight edge in drawdown control, and the lookback of 4 performs marginally better while threshold variations are negligible. For the out-of-sample configuration, I'm settling on RangeCandles=7, TP multiplier of 3.0, SL at 1.0, enabling BE with a 60% trigger (equivalent to ATRГ—1.80), enabling SPS with lookback=4 and threshold=0.3. There's also a bug fix needed in v3.1 where the BE ATR guard comparison needs to switch from SL to TP multiplier, and the optimization process itself needs adjustment.

summaries.summary: Architecting comprehensive dashboard visualization with validated findings.

content.text: # Imagine вҖ” Visual Creation Suite ## Modules Call read_me again with the modules parameter to load detailed guidance: - `diagram` вҖ” SVG flowcharts, structural diagrams, illustrative diagrams - `mockup` вҖ” UI mockups, forms, cards, dashboards - `interactive` вҖ” interactive explainers with controls - `chart` вҖ” charts, data analysis, geographic maps (Chart.js, D3 choropleth) - `art` вҖ” illustration and generative art Pick the closest fit. The module includes all relevant design guidance. **Complexity budget вҖ” hard limits:** - Box subtitles: вүӨ5 words. Detail goes in click-through (`sendPrompt`) or the prose below вҖ” not the box. - Colors: вүӨ2 ramps per diagram. If colors encode meaning (states, tiers), add a 1-line legend. Otherwise use one neutral ramp. - Horizontal tier: вүӨ4 boxes at full width (~140px each). 5+ boxes вҶ’ shrink to вүӨ110px OR wrap to 2 rows OR split into overview + detail diagrams. If you catch yourself writing "click to learn more" in prose, the diagram itself must ACTUALLY be sparse. Don't promise brevity then front-load everything. You create rich visual content вҖ” SVG diagrams/illustrations and HTML interactive widgets вҖ” that renders inline in conversation. The best output feels like a natural extension of the chat. ## Core Design System These rules apply to ALL use cases. ### Philosophy - **Seamless**: Users shouldn't notice where claude.ai ends and your widget begins. - **Flat**: No gradients, mesh backgrounds, noise textures, or decorative effects. Clean flat surfaces. - **Compact**: Show the essential inline. Explain the rest in text. - **Text goes in your response, visuals go in the tool** вҖ” All explanatory text, descriptions, introductions, and summaries must be written as normal response text OUTSIDE the tool call. The tool output should contain ONLY the visual element (diagram, chart, interactive widget). Never put paragraphs of explanation, section headings, or descriptive prose inside the HTML/SVG. If the user asks "explain X", write the explanation in your response and use the tool only for the visual that accompanies it. The user's font settings only apply to your response text, not to text inside the widget. ### Streaming Output streams token-by-token. Structure code so useful content appears early. - **HTML**: `

Now I have comprehensive data. Let me analyze the key findings:

1. **TP_ATR_Mult**: Clear trend - TPГ—3.0 dominates. Mean PF consistently improves as TP_mult increases. TPГ—3.0 has the most viables (2863) and highest max (1.397).

2. **SL_ATR_Mult**: SLГ—1.0 is overwhelmingly dominant (8007 viables vs 543 for SLГ—1.5). This is very clear.

3. **RangeCandles**: The cross table shows RangeCandles=7 and 8 with TPГ—2.5-3.0 form a strong plateau. RangeCandles=4 also works well.
   
4. **BE_TriggerPct**, **SPS_Lookback**, and **SPS_Threshold** all show minimal variation across their ranges - these parameters have negligible impact on performance. SPS performs marginally better when enabled versus disabled (1.256 vs 1.252 median), while BE OFF edges out BE ON on median (1.249 vs 1.242), though BE ON achieves higher peaks.

9. **Robustness analysis** reveals the top performers consistently cluster around TPГ—3.0, SLГ—1.0, and RangeCandles of either 4 or 7, with neighbor PF hovering around 1.16 and nearly all neighbors profitable - a strong, stable plateau. The critical takeaway is that TPГ—3.0 dominates with a wide plateau, SLГ—1.0 is nearly universal among viable configurations, RangeCandles has two equally effective settings, and BE trigger percentage is completely flat across all values, which is particularly relevant given the shift to ATR-based BE in v3.1. SPS impact is negligible whether on or off.

Since this is v3.0 with the percentage-based BE trigger, the flatness across BE_TriggerPct values (30-70) actually validates the v3.1 redesign - the specific percentage doesn't drive results, only whether BE is active matters, and even that's marginal. For translating to v3.1, I should prioritize InpRangeCandles at 4 or 7, InpTP_ATR_Mult at 3.0, and InpSL_ATR_Mult at 1.0 for the top tier, with BE_ATR_Mult considerations for tier 2.

Now I'm realizing the backtest spans 2022-2026, which is a full four-year in-sample period - longer than typical - so I need to reconsider the out-of-sample strategy accordingly. Now I need to give a clear, actionable text analysis of the results. Let me focus on the key takeaways and what to do next for v3.1.---

## QuГ© dicen los datos вҖ” anГЎlisis directo

**El borde real estГЎ en TPГ—3.0 / SLГ—1.0.** Nada mГЎs. El PF crece monotГіnicamente de 1.0x a 3.0x вҖ” no hay pico aislado ni sobreajuste, es una tendencia limpia en toda la superficie. El SLГ—1.0 aplasta a SLГ—1.5 (8,007 viables vs 543). Estos dos valores no se tocan en v3.1.

**Dos familias de `RangeCandles` funcionan:** rango corto de 4 velas (20 min) y rango largo de 7вҖ“8 velas (35вҖ“40 min). No se solapan en el cross-table вҖ” son dos lГіgicas distintas del mercado. Para v3.1 conviene correr OOS con ambos y comparar.

**`InpBE_TriggerPct` es estadГӯsticamente plano.** De 30 a 70 el PF varГӯa solo 0.007. Esto valida directamente el cambio a `InpBE_ATR_Mult` en v3.1 вҖ” el % fijo no captura ninguna seГұal Гәtil. La versiГіn ATR serГЎ mГЎs expresiva para el optimizer.

**Una advertencia importante:** el XML corre IS desde **2022.01.01 hasta 2026.03.31** вҖ” eso es prГЎcticamente 4.25 aГұos de IS sin ventana OOS separada. El optimizer vio todo, incluido 2025 y parte de 2026. Para la validaciГіn OOS de v3.1 necesitas correr una single-run en un perГӯodo que el tester **no haya visto**, que en este caso serГӯa solo el forward en demo desde hoy.

---

## ParГЎmetros fijos para la optimizaciГіn v3.1

Con estos resultados, los Tier 1 y 2 de v3.1 quedan ajustados:

**Tier 1 вҖ” ya confirmado, reducir rango:**

| ParГЎmetro | Start | Step | Stop | RazГіn |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `InpRangeCandles` | 4 | 3 | 7 | Solo testear los dos plateaus: 4 y 7 |
| `InpTP_ATR_Mult` | 2.5 | 0.5 | 3.0 | Eliminar 1.0вҖ“2.0, confirmados dГ©biles |
| `InpSL_ATR_Mult` | 0.5 | 0.5 | 1.5 | SLГ—2.0 innecesario |

**Tier 2 вҖ” `InpBE_ATR_Mult` ahora tiene contexto:**

Con `SLГ—1.0` confirmado, los rangos del BE tienen sentido econГіmico directo: `BEГ—0.3` = trigger a 30% del SL, `BEГ—0.9` = trigger a 90% del SL. El optimizer de v3.1 tiene **informaciГіn que v3.0 no tenГӯa** porque el eje es ahora comparable con el SL en la misma unidad.

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import re
import pandas as pd

path = "/mnt/user-data/uploads/Optimizer-11935103.xml"

with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()

rows = re.findall(r'<Row>(.*?)</Row>', content, re.DOTALL)

def extract_cells(row_str):
    return re.findall(r'<Data[^>]*>([^<]*)</Data>', row_str)

header = extract_cells(rows[0])
data = []
for row in rows[1:]:
    cells = extract_cells(row)
    if len(cells) == len(header):
        data.append(cells)

df = pd.DataFrame(data, columns=header)

# Convert numerics
num_cols = ['Pass','Profit','Expected Payoff','Profit Factor','Recovery Factor',
            'Sharpe Ratio','Equity DD %','Trades','InpRangeCandles',
            'InpTP_ATR_Mult','InpSL_ATR_Mult','InpBE_TriggerPct',
            'InpSPS_Lookback','InpSPS_Threshold']
for c in num_cols:
    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors='coerce')

df['InpBE_Enabled'] = df['InpBE_Enabled'].map({'true':True,'false':False})
df['InpSPS_Enabled'] = df['InpSPS_Enabled'].map({'true':True,'false':False})

print(f"Total passes: {len(df)}")
print(f"\nProfit Factor range: {df['Profit Factor'].min():.3f} вҖ“ {df['Profit Factor'].max():.3f}")
print(f"Equity DD % range: {df['Equity DD %'].min():.2f}% вҖ“ {df['Equity DD %'].max():.2f}%")
print(f"Trades range: {df['Trades'].min():.0f} вҖ“ {df['Trades'].max():.0f}")

# Filter: viable candidates (PF > 1.20, DD < 10%, Trades > 100, Profit > 0)
viable = df[(df['Profit Factor'] > 1.20) &
            (df['Equity DD %'] < 10.0) &
            (df['Trades'] > 100) &
            (df['Profit'] > 0)].copy()

print(f"\nViables (PF>1.20, DD<10%, Trades>100, Profit>0): {len(viable)}")

# Sort by Profit Factor desc
viable = viable.sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False)

print("\n=== TOP 25 CONFIGURACIONES VIABLES ===")
print(f"{'PF':>6} {'Profit':>8} {'DD%':>6} {'Sharpe':>7} {'Trades':>7} | {'Range':>5} {'TPГ—':>5} {'SLГ—':>5} {'BE%':>4} {'SPS_Lb':>6} {'SPS_Th':>6} {'BE_On':>6}")
print("-"*100)
for _, r in viable.head(25).iterrows():

{"language": "bash", "code": "python3 << 'EOF'
import re
import pandas as pd

path = "/mnt/user-data/uploads/Optimizer-11935103.xml"

with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()

rows = re.findall(r'<Row>(.*?)</Row>', content, re.DOTALL)

def extract_cells(row_str):
    return re.findall(r'<Data[^>]*>([^<]*)</Data>', row_str)

header = extract_cells(rows[0])
data = []
for row in rows[1:]:
    cells = extract_cells(row)
    if len(cells) == len(header):
        data.append(cells)

df = pd.DataFrame(data, columns=header)

# Convert numerics
num_cols = ['Pass','Profit','Expected Payoff','Profit Factor','Recovery Factor',
            'Sharpe Ratio','Equity DD %','Trades','InpRangeCandles',
            'InpTP_ATR_Mult','InpSL_ATR_Mult','InpBE_TriggerPct',
            'InpSPS_Lookback','InpSPS_Threshold']
for c in num_cols:
    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors='coerce')

df['InpBE_Enabled'] = df['InpBE_Enabled'].map({'true':True,'false':False})
df['InpSPS_Enabled'] = df['InpSPS_Enabled'].map({'true':True,'false':False})

print(f"Total passes: {len(df)}")
print(f"\nProfit Factor range: {df['Profit Factor'].min():.3f} – {df['Profit Factor'].max():.3f}")
print(f"Equity DD % range: {df['Equity DD %'].min():.2f}% – {df['Equity DD %'].max():.2f}%")
print(f"Trades range: {df['Trades'].min():.0f} – {df['Trades'].max():.0f}")

# Filter: viable candidates (PF > 1.20, DD < 10%, Trades > 100, Profit > 0)
viable = df[(df['Profit Factor'] > 1.20) &
            (df['Equity DD %'] < 10.0) &
            (df['Trades'] > 100) &
            (df['Profit'] > 0)].copy()

print(f"\nViables (PF>1.20, DD<10%, Trades>100, Profit>0): {len(viable)}")

# Sort by Profit Factor desc
viable = viable.sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False)

print("\n=== TOP 25 CONFIGURACIONES VIABLES ===")
print(f"{'PF':>6} {'Profit':>8} {'DD%':>6} {'Sharpe':>7} {'Trades':>7} | {'Range':>5} {'TP×':>5} {'SL×':>5} {'BE%':>4} {'SPS_Lb':>6} {'SPS_Th':>6} {'BE_On':>6}")
print("-"*100)
for _, r in viable.head(25).iterrows():
    print(f"{r['Profit Factor']:6.3f} {r['Profit']:8.2f} {r['Equity DD %']:6.2f} {r['Sharpe Ratio']:7.3f} {r['Trades']:7.0f} | {r['InpRangeCandles']:5.0f} {r['InpTP_ATR_Mult']:5.1f} {r['InpSL_ATR_Mult']:5.1f} {r['InpBE_TriggerPct']:4.0f} {r['InpSPS_Lookback']:6.0f} {r['InpSPS_Threshold']:6.2f} {str(r['InpBE_Enabled']):>6}")

# Save for later analysis
df.to_pickle('/home/claude/opt_df.pkl')
viable.to_pickle('/home/claude/opt_viable.pkl')
print(f"\nDataframes saved.")
EOF
"}

python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df   = pd.read_pickle('/home/claude/opt_df.pkl')
viab = pd.read_pickle('/home/claude/opt_viable.pkl')

# в”Җв”Җв”Җ 1. PLATEAU ANALYSIS por parГЎmetro individual в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("в•җ"*60)
print("PLATEAU POR InpTP_ATR_Mult (todos los viables)")
print("в•җ"*60)
grp = viab.groupby('InpTP_ATR_Mult')['Profit Factor'].agg(['mean','median','count','max'])
print(grp.round(3))

print("\n" + "в•җ"*60)
print("PLATEAU POR InpSL_ATR_Mult")
print("в•җ"*60)
grp2 = viab.groupby('InpSL_ATR_Mult')['Profit Factor'].agg(['mean','median','count','max'])
print(grp2.round(3))

print("\n" + "в•җ"*60)
print("PLATEAU POR InpRangeCandles")
print("в•җ"*60)
grp3 = viab.groupby('InpRangeCandles')['Profit Factor'].agg(['mean','median','count','max'])
print(grp3.round(3))

print("\n" + "в•җ"*60)
print("PLATEAU POR InpBE_TriggerPct")
print("в•җ"*60)
grp4 = viab.groupby('InpBE_TriggerPct')['Profit Factor'].agg(['mean','median','count','max'])
print(grp4.round(3))

print("\n" + "в•җ"*60)
print("PLATEAU POR InpSPS_Lookback")
print("в•җ"*60)
grp5 = viab.groupby('InpSPS_Lookback')['Profit Factor'].agg(['mean','median','count','max'])
print(grp5.round(3))

print("\n" + "в•җ"*60)
print("PLATEAU POR InpSPS_Threshold")
print("в•җ"*60)
grp6 = viab.groupby('InpSPS_Threshold')['Profit Factor'].agg(['mean','median','count','max'])
print(grp6.round(3))

# в”Җв”Җв”Җ 2. SPS ON vs OFF в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("\n" + "в•җ"*60)
print("SPS ON vs OFF вҖ” impacto")
print("в•җ"*60)
sps_grp = viab.groupby('InpSPS_Enabled')['Profit Factor'].agg(['mean','median','count','max'])
print(sps_grp.round(3))

# в”Җв”Җв”Җ 3. BE ON vs OFF в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("\n" + "в•җ"*60)
print("BE ON vs OFF вҖ” impacto")
print("в•җ"*60)
be_grp = viab.groupby('InpBE_Enabled')['Profit Factor'].agg(['mean','median','count','max'])
print(be_grp.round(3))

# в”Җв”Җв”Җ 4. CROSS TABLE: RangeCandles vs TP_Mult в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
print("\n" + "в•җ"*60)
print("CROSS: RangeCandles Г— TP_ATR_Mult  (median PF de viables)")
print("в•җ"*60)
cross = viab.pivot_table(values='Profit Factor', 
                          index='InpRangeCandles', 
                          columns='InpTP_ATR_Mult', 
                          aggfunc='median')
print(cross.round(3))

# в”Җв”Җв”Җ 5. MEJOR CONFIGURACIГ“N ROBUSTA (plateau, no pico) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
# Criterio: promedio PF de vecinos (Вұ1 step en cada parГЎmetro)
print("\n" + "в•җ"*60)
print("TOP 10 CONFIGURACIONES CON MAYOR ROBUSTEZ DE VECINDAD")
print("(PF propio + promedio PF vecinos Вұ1 step)")
print("в•җ"*60)

top = viab.head(50).copy()
robustness_scores = []

for _, r in top.iterrows():
    rc = r['InpRangeCandles']
    tp = r['InpTP_ATR_Mult']
    sl = r['InpSL_ATR_Mult']
    be = r['InpBE_TriggerPct']
    lb = r['InpSPS_Lookback']
    th = r['InpSPS_Threshold']
    
    # Vecinos: variar Вұ1 step cada parГЎmetro
    neighbors = df[
        (df['InpRangeCandles'].isin([rc-2, rc, rc+2])) &
        (df['InpTP_ATR_Mult'].isin([round(tp-0.5,1), tp, round(tp+0.5,1)])) &
        (df['InpSL_ATR_Mult'].isin([round(sl-0.5,1), sl, round(sl+0.5,1)])) &
        (df['InpBE_TriggerPct'].isin([be-5, be, be+5])) &
        (df['InpSPS_Lookback'].isin([lb-4, lb, lb+4])) &
        (df['InpSPS_Threshold'].isin([round(th-0.1,2), th, round(th+0.1,2)]))
    ]
    
    n_neighbors = len(neighbors)
    avg_neighbor_pf = neighbors['Profit Factor'].mean() if n_neighbors > 0 else 0
    pct_profitable_neighbors = (neighbors['Profit Factor'] > 1.0).mean() * 100 if n_neighbors > 0 else 0
    
    robustness_scores.append({
        'own_PF':   r['Profit Factor'],
        'profit':   r['Profit'],
        'dd':       r['Equity DD %'],
        'trades':   int(r['Trades']),
        'sharpe':   r['Sharpe Ratio'],
        'neighbor_PF': avg_neighbor_pf,
        'n_neighbors': n_neighbors,
        'pct_prof_neigh': pct_profitable_neighbors,
        'robustness': (r['Profit Factor'] + avg_neighbor_pf) / 2,
        'RangeC': rc, 'TP': tp, 'SL': sl, 'BE': be, 'LB': lb, 'TH': th,
        'BE_on': r['InpBE_Enabled']
    })

rob_df = pd.DataFrame(robustness_scores).sort_values('robustness', ascending=False)
print(f"\n{'Rob':>6} {'OwnPF':>6} {'NbrPF':>6} {'%ProfN':>7} {'Profit':>8} {'DD%':>5} {'Trades':>7} | {'Rng':>4} {'TP':>4} {'SL':>4} {'BE%':>4} {'LB':>4} {'TH':>5}")
print("-"*100)
for _, r in rob_df.head(10).iterrows():
    print(f"{r['robustness']:6.3f} {r['own_PF']:6.3f} {r['neighbor_PF']:6.3f} {r['pct_prof_neigh']:7.1f} {r['profit']:8.2f} {r['dd']:5.2f} {r['trades']:7d} | {r['RangeC']:4.0f} {r['TP']:4.1f} {r['SL']:4.1f} {r['BE']:4.0f} {r['LB']:4.0f} {r['TH']:5.2f}")

EOF

content.thinking:
Now I have comprehensive data. Let me analyze the key findings:

1. **TP_ATR_Mult**: Clear trend - TPГ—3.0 dominates. Mean PF consistently improves as TP_mult increases. TPГ—3.0 has the most viables (2863) and highest max (1.397).

2. **SL_ATR_Mult**: SLГ—1.0 is overwhelmingly dominant (8007 viables vs 543 for SLГ—1.5). This is very clear.

3. **RangeCandles**: The cross table shows RangeCandles=7 and 8 with TPГ—2.5-3.0 form a strong plateau. RangeCandles=4 also works well.
   
4. **BE_TriggerPct**, **SPS_Lookback**, and **SPS_Threshold** all show minimal variation across their ranges - these parameters have negligible impact on performance. SPS performs marginally better when enabled versus disabled (1.256 vs 1.252 median), while BE OFF edges out BE ON on median (1.249 vs 1.242), though BE ON achieves higher peaks.

9. **Robustness analysis** reveals the top performers consistently cluster around TPГ—3.0, SLГ—1.0, and RangeCandles of either 4 or 7, with neighbor PF hovering around 1.16 and nearly all neighbors profitable - a strong, stable plateau. The critical takeaway is that TPГ—3.0 dominates with a wide plateau, SLГ—1.0 is nearly universal among viable configurations, RangeCandles has two equally effective settings, and BE trigger percentage is completely flat across all values, which is particularly relevant given the shift to ATR-based BE in v3.1. SPS impact is negligible whether on or off.

Since this is v3.0 with the percentage-based BE trigger, the flatness across BE_TriggerPct values (30-70) actually validates the v3.1 redesign - the specific percentage doesn't drive results, only whether BE is active matters, and even that's marginal. For translating to v3.1, I should prioritize InpRangeCandles at 4 or 7, InpTP_ATR_Mult at 3.0, and InpSL_ATR_Mult at 1.0 for the top tier, with BE_ATR_Mult considerations for tier 2.

Now I'm realizing the backtest spans 2022-2026, which is a full four-year in-sample period - longer than typical - so I need to reconsider the out-of-sample strategy accordingly. Let me build out a comprehensive dashboard to visualize all these relationships.

The module includes all relevant design guidance. **Complexity budget вҖ” hard limits:** - Box subtitles: вүӨ5 words. Detail goes in click-through (`sendPrompt`) or the prose below вҖ” not the box. - Colors: вүӨ2 ramps per diagram. If colors encode meaning (states, tiers), add a 1-line legend. Otherwise use one neutral ramp. - Horizontal tier: вүӨ4 boxes at full width (~140px each). 5+ boxes вҶ’ shrink to вүӨ110px OR wrap to 2 rows OR split into overview + detail diagrams. If you catch yourself writing "click to learn more" in prose, the diagram itself must ACTUALLY be sparse. Don't promise brevity then front-load everything. You create rich visual content вҖ” SVG diagrams/illustrations and HTML interactive widgets вҖ” that renders inline in conversation. The best output feels like a natural extension of the chat. ## Core Design System These rules apply to ALL use cases. ### Philosophy - **Seamless**: Users shouldn't notice where claude.ai ends and your widget begins. - **Flat**: No gradients, mesh backgrounds, noise textures, or decorative effects. Clean flat surfaces. - **Compact**: Show the essential inline. Explain the rest in text. - **Text goes in your response, visuals go in the tool** вҖ” All explanatory text, descriptions, introductions, and summaries must be written as normal response text OUTSIDE the tool call. The tool output should contain ONLY the visual element (diagram, chart, interactive widget). Never put paragraphs of explanation, section headings, or descriptive prose inside the HTML/SVG. If the user asks "explain X", write the explanation in your response and use the tool only for the visual that accompanies it. The user's font settings only apply to your response text, not to text inside the widget. ### Streaming Output streams token-by-token. Structure code so useful content appears early. - **HTML**: `

Conclusiones clave para v3.1
●
TPГ—3.0 y SLГ—1.0 son los unicos ganadores. El SLГ—1.5 y 2.0 casi no aparecen en viables. El TP crece monotonicamente вҖ” no hay riesgo de sobreajuste en este eje. Estos valores van fijos para el OOS.
●
Dos plateaus en RangeCandles: 4 y 7вҖ“8. El rango de 4 velas (20 min) y el de 7вҖ“8 (35вҖ“40 min) son igualmente robustos. Para v3.1 se recomienda testear ambos en OOS por separado y quedarse con el mas consistente.
●
BE_TriggerPct es practicamente irrelevante. La mediana PF va de 1.249 (pct=30) a 1.256 (pct=70) вҖ” diferencia de 0.007. Esto confirma que mover a BE basado en ATR en v3.1 es correcto: el % fijo no aporta informacion estadistica.
●
SPS y BE aportan marginalmente. SPS ON = +0.004 PF. BE OFF = +0.007 PF en mediana. Ninguno es determinante. El borde real esta en TPГ—3.0 / SLГ—1.0. El SPS y BE son refinamientos de control de riesgo, no generadores de borde.
●
Alerta IS extendido. El XML corre 2022-2026 (4 anos de IS). Esto da mucha confianza estadistica pero el OOS disponible es solo 2026 en adelante. Usar 2025 como OOS parcial y 2026 como forward test real.
